title: Qualys VMDR - Host Detection And Reporting Conventions
applies_to: Qualys VMDR host inventory and vulnerability detections
Qualys vulnerability reporting derived from classic VM APIs

confirmed_facts.fact: The Host Detection List API is GET or POST /api/2.0/fo/asset/host/vm/detection/?action=list.
Qualys describes the Host Detection List API as a source of the latest complete vulnerability status for hosts, including NEW, ACTIVE, FIXED, and REOPENED, plus history information.
The Host Detection List API can include the Qualys Detection Score (QDS) in output and can filter records by QDS.
The API supports output_format values XML, CSV, CSV_NO_METADATA, CSV_NO_METADATA_MS_EXCEL, and CSV_MS_EXCEL, with XML as the default.
The API supports host-side scoping and time filters such as detection_updated_since/before, vm_processed_after/before, and asset tag-related parameters when tag-based scoping is used.

Qualys UI documentation for Vulnerability Details lists QID, Title, QDS, and Severity as core vulnerability-view columns, which aligns with deriving dashboard-style metrics from host detections plus KnowledgeBase enrichment.
tag_set_exclude REQUIRES tag_set_include to be present. Using tag_set_exclude alone (without tag_set_include) returns error 1903. Always supply a tag_set_include alongside any tag_set_exclude.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — Qualys tag filtering confirmed; error 1903 reproduced and resolved

confirmed_facts.fact: Qualys tag names are case-sensitive. Using 'info: Managed by Digital Services' (lowercase i) returns error 1905. The correct tag name is 'Info: Managed by Digital Services' (capital I).
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — tag name case error 1905 observed and corrected

confirmed_facts.fact: Confirmed working tag names in this environment (as of April 2026): 'External IPs', 'Info: Managed by Digital Services', 'Info: Managed by Faculity' (note: 'Faculity' is a canonical typo in the Qualys tenant — do not correct it in queries). The tag 'Internal IPs' did not exist at time of testing and must be created manually in the Qualys console before use.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — tag-filtered queries confirmed working with these exact names

confirmed_facts.fact: Basic Auth (per-request Authorization: Basic base64(user:pass)) does not consume Qualys concurrent session slots. Session-based auth (login/logout flow) consumes the 2-session limit and causes error 2014 when multiple requests run concurrently. Use Basic Auth for Logic Apps and automation workflows.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — Basic Auth confirmed working without session exhaustion; session-based auth caused error 2014

confirmed_facts.fact: The complete authoritative parameter allowlist for the Host Detection API action=list (confirmed from API error 1901 response, April 2026): host scoping: ips, ids, id_min, id_max, ag_ids, ag_titles, os_pattern, network_ids, use_tags, show_tags, show_asset_id, show_results, show_host_services, show_cloud_tags, cloud_tag_fields, host_metadata, host_metadata_fields, compliance_enabled; detection filtering: status, severities, qids, show_igs, include_ignored, include_disabled, active_kernels_only, filter_superseded_qids, show_reopened_info, include_vuln_type, include_search_list_titles, exclude_search_list_titles, include_search_list_ids, exclude_search_list_ids; QDS: show_qds, qds_min, qds_max, show_qds_factors; time filters: no_vm_scan_since, vm_scan_since, vm_processed_after, vm_processed_before, vm_scan_date_before, vm_scan_date_after, vm_auth_scan_date_before, vm_auth_scan_date_after, max_days_since_last_vm_scan, max_days_since_detection_updated, detection_last_tested_since_days, detection_last_tested_before_days, detection_updated_since, detection_updated_before, detection_processed_after, detection_processed_before, detection_last_tested_since, detection_last_tested_before; ARF: arf_kernel_filter, arf_service_filter, arf_config_filter, arf_filter_keys, show_arf_data; output: output_format, truncation_limit, suppress_duplicated_data_from_csv, echo_request; other: mitre_attack_details.
confirmed_facts.source: API error 1901 response — complete allowed parameter list returned by Qualys when unrecognized parameters were submitted

confirmed_facts.fact: Common wrong parameter names for action=list and their correct equivalents: show_disabled_flag -> include_disabled, exclude_non_running_kernels -> active_kernels_only, show_superseded_patches -> filter_superseded_qids. All three wrong names return error 1901. Correct exclusion filter set: show_igs=0 (ignored), include_disabled=0 (disabled), active_kernels_only=1 (non-running kernels), filter_superseded_qids=1 (superseded patches), status=New,Active,Re-Opened (fixed).
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — error 1901 confirmed wrong names; correct names validated against API allowlist

implementation_notes: Treat host detections as the fact table for vulnerability reporting. Derive dashboard metrics from detection rows, not from a dedicated dashboard API surface.
Use show_asset_id=1 so the classic detection feed can be joined directly to Asset Inventory assetId values when available.
Enable show_qds=1 whenever reporting requires QDS-based filtering or bucketing.
Use detection_updated_since/before for incremental change capture and vm_processed_after/before when you need report windows tied to host processing time.
Expect XML parsing for the richest classic workflow. CSV exports can help with ad hoc extraction, but XML preserves nested host and detection structures more predictably.
Use truncation_limit deliberately and follow the continuation URL in truncated responses for large host scopes.
caveats: The documented sources do not expose one dedicated VMDR dashboard metrics API. Snapshot, monthly, and quarterly metrics must be computed from detections, KnowledgeBase enrichment, and asset inventory data.
QDS is detection-centric and distinct from KB severity. Keep both in the normalized model because users may ask for either severity or QDS views.
Tag-based scoping and host visibility depend on user permissions and Qualys scoping features such as TBUS.
